name: edge: copy with nothing selected leaves the clipboard unchanged
comment: Verifies that Copy with no selection is a no-op that does NOT clear the clipboard. Uses AutoPilot's target-less `clipboard` assertion (reads the system pasteboard directly): type a known marker, select-all, copy -> assert clipboard contains the marker; then deselect (caret move) and press cmd+c with NOTHING selected (the no-op under test) -> assert the clipboard STILL contains the marker. tryToBreakIt: the second assert is the real check - a wrong implementation that cleared the pasteboard on an empty copy would fail it. Previously this had to paste into a new tab and read the editor because there was no clipboard-read primitive (AutoPilot feedback D6, now closed on feature/ap-feedback). Documented primitives only. Requires the Debug build. No fixture; starts from an empty untitled document.
